items.title: Elon Musk gets an apology from California regulators as SpaceX lawsuit is settled
items.standfirst: Regulators settled suit that claimed a state agency showed political bias against rocket company and its chief executiveSign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inboxCalifornia regulators apologized to the SpaceX CEO, Elon Musk, this week as they settled a lawsuit that claimed a state agency showed political bias against the rocket company and its chief executive.As part of the settlement, the California coastal commission acknowledged its members made “improper” statements about Musk’s political beliefs at a 2024 hearing on SpaceX’s Falcon 9 launch program. items.title: Jerome Powell says he’ll stay on Fed board after central bank keeps interest rates unchanged in defiance of Trump
items.standfirst: Chair had said he’d leave after inquiry into building renovations but now says there are ‘remaining steps in the process’ he’s watchingSign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inboxThe US Federal Reserve chair, Jerome Powell, is staying on the central bank’s rate-setting board after his term as chair ends in May, a contentious move that signals continued uncertainty at the Fed.Powell made the announcement after the Fed board left interest rates unchanged for the third time this year on Wednesday, despite Donald Trump’s continued demands for interest rate cuts. Continue reading...

clusters.title: ‘A devastating blow’: major civil rights group calls supreme court ruling on voting case ‘a major setback for our nation’ – live

bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian World, Al Jazeera, CBS News and others

items.title: ‘A devastating blow’: major civil rights group calls supreme court ruling on voting case ‘a major setback for our nation’ – live
items.standfirst: NAACP decries 6-3 decision that ruled Louisiana must redraw its congressional map, a landmark case that guts major section of Voting Rights ActSign up for the Breaking News US emailUS supreme court ‘demolishes’ key Voting Rights Act provision that prevented racial discriminationThe US Federal Reserve is widely expected to hold interest rates steady on Wednesday after a key policy meeting, likely the last chaired by central bank chief Jerome Powell, a frequent target of president Donald Trump’s ire.Policymakers will weigh the risks of surging energy prices and snarled supply chains due to the US-Israel war on Iran, with analysts widely expecting a third pause in a row as the effects of the conflict ripple through the world’s largest economy. Continue reading...
items.content: NAACP decries 6-3 decision that ruled Louisiana must redraw its congressional map, a landmark case that guts major section of Voting Rights ActSign up for the Breaking News US emailUS supreme court ‘demolishes’ key Voting Rights Act provision that prevented racial discriminationThe US Federal Reserve is widely expected to hold interest rates steady on Wednesday after a key policy meeting, likely the last chaired by central bank chief Jerome Powell, a frequent target of president Donald Trump’s ire.Policymakers will weigh the risks of surging energy prices and snarled supply chains due to the US-Israel war on Iran, with analysts widely expecting a third pause in a row as the effects of the conflict ripple through the world’s largest economy. Continue reading...

items.title: US supreme court ‘demolishes’ Voting Rights Act, gutting provision that prevented racial discrimination
items.standfirst: Justices rule in landmark decision Louisiana must redraw congressional map, largely killing major civil rights lawUS politics live – latest updatesSign up for the Breaking News US newsletter emailThe US supreme court has ruled that Louisiana will have to redraw its congressional map, in a landmark decision that effectively guts a major section of the Voting Rights Act.In a 6-3 decision along partisan lines, the court rendered ineffective section 2 of the Voting Rights Act, the last remaining powerful provision of the 1965 civil rights law that prevents racial discrimination in voting. Section 2 has long been used to ensure minority voters are treated fairly in redistricting. Continue reading...
items.content: Justices rule in landmark decision Louisiana must redraw congressional map, largely killing major civil rights lawUS politics live – latest updatesSign up for the Breaking News US newsletter emailThe US supreme court has ruled that Louisiana will have to redraw its congressional map, in a landmark decision that effectively guts a major section of the Voting Rights Act.In a 6-3 decision along partisan lines, the court rendered ineffective section 2 of the Voting Rights Act, the last remaining powerful provision of the 1965 civil rights law that prevents racial discrimination in voting. Section 2 has long been used to ensure minority voters are treated fairly in redistricting. Continue reading...

items.title: US top court voids Louisiana voting map amid national redistricting fight
items.standfirst: Ruling seen as major blow to US Voting Rights Act, with critics saying it could dilute minority voting power.
items.content: Ruling seen as major blow to US Voting Rights Act, with critics saying it could dilute minority voting power.

items.title: Supreme Court weakens Voting Rights Act, voiding Louisiana congressional map
items.standfirst: The Supreme Court rule 6-3 in a decision that has implications for the scope of the landmark Voting Rights Act.
items.content: The Supreme Court rule 6-3 in a decision that has implications for the scope of the landmark Voting Rights Act.

items.title: US Supreme Court limits use of race in drawing electoral maps
items.standfirst: The decision will make it more difficult to successfully challenge legislative maps for diluting the voting power of racial minorities.
items.content: The decision will make it more difficult to successfully challenge legislative maps for diluting the voting power of racial minorities.

items.title: Supreme Court calls Louisiana's House map an 'unconstitutional racial gerrymander'
items.standfirst: The court kept Section 2 of the Voting Rights Act intact, but the decision all but guts a landmark law that came out of the Civil Rights Movement and protected the collective voting power of racial minorities when political maps are redrawn.
items.content: The court kept Section 2 of the Voting Rights Act intact, but the decision all but guts a landmark law that came out of the Civil Rights Movement and protected the collective voting power of racial minorities when political maps are redrawn.(Image credit: Kevin Dietsch)

items.title: Two wounded as knife attacker targets London’s Jewish community
items.standfirst: U.K. police declared a stabbing attack that injured two Jewish people in London was a "terrorist incident." A 45-year-old man with a history of mental health issues stabbed and seriously wounded two Jewish men in Golders Green, North London.
items.content: U.K. police declared a stabbing attack that injured two Jewish people in London was a "terrorist incident." A 45-year-old man with a history of mental health issues stabbed and seriously wounded two Jewish men in Golders Green, North London.

items.title: Watch: How the Golders Green attack unfolded
items.standfirst: Two Jewish men were been stabbed in Golders Green, north London, as the Metropolitan Police formally declared it a terrorist incident.
items.content: Two Jewish men were been stabbed in Golders Green, north London, as the Metropolitan Police formally declared it a terrorist incident.

items.title: London police say stabbing of 2 Jewish men is declared a terrorist incident
items.standfirst: Two Jewish men were stabbed and injured in a London street on Wednesday, in what police called an act of terrorism. Police arrested a 45-year-old man on suspicion of attempted murder in the city's latest antisemitic attack. The Metropolitan Police said the attack in the Golders Green area left two men, aged 34 and 76, hospitalized with knife wounds.
items.content: Two Jewish men were stabbed and injured in a London street on Wednesday, in what police called an act of terrorism. Police arrested a 45-year-old man on suspicion of attempted murder in the city's latest antisemitic attack. The Metropolitan Police said the attack in the Golders Green area left two men, aged 34 and 76, hospitalized with knife wounds.

items.title: Police declare terrorist incident after 2 Jewish men stabbed in London
items.standfirst: London's mayor says police will increase patrols after an "appalling attack on two Jewish Londoners," amid a "series of shocking antisemitic attacks" in the city.
items.content: London's mayor says police will increase patrols after an "appalling attack on two Jewish Londoners," amid a "series of shocking antisemitic attacks" in the city.

items.title: Stabbing of two Jewish people in London declared a terrorist incident
items.standfirst: LONDON — Police declared a Wednesday stabbing attack that injured two Jewish people in London a "terrorist incident."
items.content: LONDON — Police declared a Wednesday stabbing attack that injured two Jewish people in London a "terrorist incident."

items.title: James Comey surrenders to authorities after indictment over seashell post
items.standfirst: Indictment claims ex-FBI director’s social media post last year of shells arranged into ‘86 47’ was threat against TrumpUS politics live – latest updatesJames Comey made a brief appearance in court on Wednesday after the justice department indicted him over a social media post in a renewed bid to prosecute one of Donald Trump’s longtime political adversaries.The former FBI director was indicted in North Carolina on Tuesday over a photograph he posted last year of seashells arranged in the numbers “86 47” – a message the justice department says amounts to a threat against Trump, the 47th US president. items.title: Seven lawsuits filed against OpenAI by families of Canada mass-shooting victims
items.standfirst: The lawsuits, filed in California, accuse OpenAI and Sam Altman of negligence and abetting a mass shooting by failing to flag the suspect's ChatGPT activity.
items.content: The lawsuits, filed in California, accuse OpenAI and Sam Altman of negligence and abetting a mass shooting by failing to flag the suspect's ChatGPT activity.

items.title: OpenAI Sued by Families of Canada School Shooting Victims
items.standfirst: OpenAI is the target of new lawsuits over the mass shooting in Tumbler Ridge, British Columbia, that allege the artificial intelligence company could have stopped the suspected killer from using its popular chatbot, ChatGPT, ahead of the attack.
items.content: OpenAI is the target of new lawsuits over the mass shooting in Tumbler Ridge, British Columbia, that allege the artificial intelligence company could have stopped the suspected killer from using its popular chatbot, ChatGPT, ahead of the attack.

items.title: Families sue OpenAI over failure to report Canada mass shooter’s behavior on ChatGPT
items.standfirst: New lawsuits allege employees urged company to notify authorities months before deadly Tumbler Ridge attackFamilies of seven victims of a mass shooting at a secondary school in British Columbia are suing OpenAI and the company’s CEO for negligence after it failed to alert authorities to the shooter’s troubling conversations with ChatGPT.The lawsuits, filed on Wednesday in a federal court in San Francisco, allege that the violent intentions of the shooter, identified as 18-year-old Jesse Van Rootselaar, were well-known to OpenAI. Employees at the company flagged the shooter’s account eight months before the attack and determined that it posed “a credible and specific threat of gun violence against real people”, according to the lawsuit. Continue reading...
items.content: New lawsuits allege employees urged company to notify authorities months before deadly Tumbler Ridge attackFamilies of seven victims of a mass shooting at a secondary school in British Columbia are suing OpenAI and the company’s CEO for negligence after it failed to alert authorities to the shooter’s troubling conversations with ChatGPT.The lawsuits, filed on Wednesday in a federal court in San Francisco, allege that the violent intentions of the shooter, identified as 18-year-old Jesse Van Rootselaar, were well-known to OpenAI. Employees at the company flagged the shooter’s account eight months before the attack and determined that it posed “a credible and specific threat of gun violence against real people”, according to the lawsuit. Continue reading...

clusters.title: US supreme court conservatives seem to favor ending TPS for Haitians and Syrians

bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian World, CBS News, NPR News

items.title: US supreme court conservatives seem to favor ending TPS for Haitians and Syrians
items.standfirst: Nine justices were hearing Trump administration that it has authority to strip immigrants’ temporary protected status The US supreme court heard oral arguments on Wednesday over whether the Trump administration can strip the temporary protected status (TPS) of hundreds of thousands of immigrant Haitians and Syrians, under a program that has shielded them from deportation owing to safety concerns in their countries of origin.During the arguments, justices in the conservative-leaning majority appeared sympathetic to the Trump administration’s attempts to strip humanitarian protections for the Syrians and Haitians in this case. Continue reading...
items.content: Nine justices were hearing Trump administration that it has authority to strip immigrants’ temporary protected status The US supreme court heard oral arguments on Wednesday over whether the Trump administration can strip the temporary protected status (TPS) of hundreds of thousands of immigrant Haitians and Syrians, under a program that has shielded them from deportation owing to safety concerns in their countries of origin.During the arguments, justices in the conservative-leaning majority appeared sympathetic to the Trump administration’s attempts to strip humanitarian protections for the Syrians and Haitians in this case. Continue reading...

items.title: South Africa deports Mugabe’s son for unrelated offences after employee shot at family home
items.standfirst: Bellarmine Chatunga Mugabe also fined after pleading guilty to immigration and firearms-related offencesTwo months after an employee was shot in the back at the Mugabe family home in a wealthy suburb of Johannesburg, a South African court has fined and ordered the deportation of Robert Mugabe’s youngest son over two unrelated charges.Bellarmine Chatunga Mugabe, 28, and his cousin Tobias Mugabe Matonhodze, 33, were initially both charged with attempted murder after the incident on 19 February. clusters.title: US gas prices hit $4.23 high as Hormuz fears drive oil surge
clusters.neutral_headline: US gas prices hit $4.23 high as Hormuz fears drive oil surge
bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian World, Guardian Business

items.title: US gas prices hit $4.23 high as Hormuz fears drive oil surge
items.standfirst: Blockade threat in vital strait and Trump’s stance lift crude, pushing pump prices to highest level since 2022Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inboxAverage US gas prices have hit a new high at $4.23 a gallon, their highest since 2022 and a record since the start of the war with Iran, according to the motor club AAA.The price of Brent crude, the benchmark that influences the price of gasoline in the US, now stands at $114.60 a barrel, up nearly 25% from the recent low since mid-April. US gas prices a year ago averaged $3.16 a gallon. Continue reading...
items.content: Blockade threat in vital strait and Trump’s stance lift crude, pushing pump prices to highest level since 2022Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inboxAverage US gas prices have hit a new high at $4.23 a gallon, their highest since 2022 and a record since the start of the war with Iran, according to the motor club AAA.The price of Brent crude, the benchmark that influences the price of gasoline in the US, now stands at $114.60 a barrel, up nearly 25% from the recent low since mid-April. US gas prices a year ago averaged $3.16 a gallon. Continue reading...

items.title: US gas prices surge to highest level in four years, averaging $4.18 a gallon
items.standfirst: Oil prices surged as talks on reopening of strait of Hormuz remain gridlocked, sending prices $1 higher than last yearUS gas prices rose to their highest level in four years on Thursday, reaching an average $4.18 a gallon at the pump as US-Israeli peace talks with Iran remain at a standstill.The last time average US gas prices breached $4.15 a gallon was in April 2022, when oil prices soared shortly after Russia invaded Ukraine. Average gas prices are now $1 higher than just a year ago, when they were closer to $3.15 a gallon. Continue reading...
items.content: Oil prices surged as talks on reopening of strait of Hormuz remain gridlocked, sending prices $1 higher than last yearUS gas prices rose to their highest level in four years on Thursday, reaching an average $4.18 a gallon at the pump as US-Israeli peace talks with Iran remain at a standstill.The last time average US gas prices breached $4.15 a gallon was in April 2022, when oil prices soared shortly after Russia invaded Ukraine. Average gas prices are now $1 higher than just a year ago, when they were closer to $3.15 a gallon. Continue reading...

clusters.title: United States: Limited-edition Trump passport unveiled 🇺🇸
clusters.neutral_headline: Trump's face to feature on commemorative US passports
bullet_summary: Reported by France24, BBC World

items.title: United States: Limited-edition Trump passport unveiled 🇺🇸
items.standfirst: 🇺🇸 No sitting president has ever appeared on a #US passport. Yet the US State Department has unveiled a limited-edition version bearing Donald #Trump’s portrait to mark the 250th anniversary of the Declaration of #Independence. 🪙 A commemorative gold coin featuring his image is also set to be minted for the occasion.
items.content: 🇺🇸 No sitting president has ever appeared on a #US passport. Yet the US State Department has unveiled a limited-edition version bearing Donald #Trump’s portrait to mark the 250th anniversary of the Declaration of #Independence. 🪙 A commemorative gold coin featuring his image is also set to be minted for the occasion.

items.title: Trump's face to feature on commemorative US passports
items.standfirst: The passports will be released as part of the 250th anniversary of the US Declaration of Independence.
items.content: The passports will be released as part of the 250th anniversary of the US Declaration of Independence.

clusters.title: Russia will not display military equipment at Victory Day parade due to Ukraine threat
clusters.neutral_headline: Russia scales back Moscow Victory Day parade, blaming threat from Ukraine
bullet_summary: Russia ​will hold a scaled-back version of its Victory Day parade marking the defeat of Nazi Germany in World War II, the Kremlin said on Wednesday
The Kremlin said it was due to Ukraine's "terrorist activity"

items.title: Russia will not display military equipment at Victory Day parade due to Ukraine threat
items.standfirst: Russia ​will hold a scaled-back version of its Victory Day parade marking the defeat of Nazi Germany in World War II, the Kremlin said on Wednesday. For the first time in nearly two decades, the annual parade on Moscow's Red Square will not feature a military hardware display. The Kremlin said it was due to Ukraine's "terrorist activity".
items.content: Russia ​will hold a scaled-back version of its Victory Day parade marking the defeat of Nazi Germany in World War II, the Kremlin said on Wednesday. For the first time in nearly two decades, the annual parade on Moscow's Red Square will not feature a military hardware display. The Kremlin said it was due to Ukraine's "terrorist activity".

items.title: Russia scales back Moscow Victory Day parade, blaming threat from Ukraine
items.standfirst: Russia says the annual commemoration of Soviet victory in World War Two will not feature military vehicles or cadets.
items.content: Russia says the annual commemoration of Soviet victory in World War Two will not feature military vehicles or cadets.

clusters.title: Kim praises North Korean soldiers who blew themselves up to evade Ukraine capture
clusters.neutral_headline: Kim Jong Un praises troops who 'self-criticized' to avoid capture by Ukraine
bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian World, BBC World

items.title: Kim praises North Korean soldiers who blew themselves up to evade Ukraine capture
items.standfirst: Leader mentions for first time lengths to which troops go to avoid falling into enemy hands while fighting for RussiaKim Jong-un has praised North Korean soldiers who blew themselves up with grenades in order to avoid capture while fighting Ukrainian forces in Russia’s western Kursk region, confirming the existence of the extreme battlefield policy.Mounting evidence, including from intelligence reports and testimonies of defectors, has indicated North Korean soldiers are explicitly told to resort to self-detonation or other forms of suicide to avoid falling into enemy hands. Continue reading...

Continue reading...

items.standfirst: It confirms suspicions that North Korean soldiers are being told to detonate their grenades to kill themselves.
items.content: It confirms suspicions that North Korean soldiers are being told to detonate their grenades to kill themselves.

bullet_summary: Reported by BBC World, Wired

items.title: Why has the UAE left Opec - and why does this matter?
items.standfirst: The United Arab Emirates (UAE) is quitting oil cartel Opec after nearly 60 years of membership.
items.content: The United Arab Emirates (UAE) is quitting oil cartel Opec after nearly 60 years of membership.

items.title: UAE to Exit OPEC After Nearly 60 Years
items.standfirst: On May 1, the United Arab Emirates will end its a 59-year membership in the oil consortium, allowing it to raise output during one of the most volatile energy markets in years.
items.content: On May 1, the United Arab Emirates will end its a 59-year membership in the oil consortium, allowing it to raise output during one of the most volatile energy markets in years.
